_source: AdaptBase classification of administrative jurisdiction types. Designed to span global governance structures from national to sub-municipal level.
_note: Flat vocabulary (no hierarchy) — admin_level integer on Jurisdiction provides ordering hints. Values cover the most common forms of governed territory encountered in climate adaptation planning literature globally.

terms.name: State / Province
terms.description: First-level subnational division with significant legislative autonomy.

terms.name: Prefecture
terms.description: First-level subnational unit in systems using prefectural governance (Japan, Greece, etc.).

terms.name: Region
terms.description: Subnational administrative region, typically between state and county level.

terms.name: County
terms.description: Second- or third-level administrative division, often encompassing multiple municipalities.

terms.name: Municipality
terms.description: General-purpose local government unit — the most common jurisdiction type for city-level adaptation planning.

terms.name: Consolidated City-County
terms.description: Merged city and county government functioning as a single entity.

terms.name: Metropolitan Government
terms.description: Governing authority spanning a metropolitan area, often cross-municipal.
terms.examples: Greater London Authority, Metro Manila Development Authority, Tokyo Metropolitan Government

terms.name: Combined Authority
terms.description: Formally constituted group of local authorities governing jointly, especially in the UK devolution model.
terms.examples: Greater Manchester Combined Authority, West Midlands Combined Authority

terms.name: Regional Council
terms.description: Elected or appointed body governing at a regional level, often with planning and infrastructure powers.
terms.examples: Auckland Council, Bay of Plenty Regional Council

terms.name: Special District
terms.description: Limited-purpose government for a specific function (water, flood control, transit) within defined boundaries.
terms.examples: South Florida Water Management District, Bay Area Air Quality Management District

terms.name: Tribal Nation
terms.description: Sovereign indigenous government with jurisdiction over defined territory.
terms.examples: Navajo Nation, Lummi Nation, Torres Strait Island Regional Council

terms.name: Autonomous Region
terms.description: Subnational entity with significant self-governance, often based on ethnic, cultural, or geographic distinctiveness.
terms.examples: Hong Kong SAR, Catalonia, Kurdistan Region

terms.name: District
terms.examples: Dharavi (Mumbai), Kreuzberg (Berlin), Wards in Dar es Salaam

terms.name: Neighborhood
terms.description: Smallest recognized administrative or community-defined area within a municipality.

terms.name: Borough
terms.description: Administrative division within a larger city, with some degree of self-governance.

terms.name: Other
terms.description: Jurisdiction type not captured by the above categories. Use sparingly and note the specific type in entity description.
terms.examples: Free economic zone, military district, papal territory
